name: Stir-Fried Pork and Asparagus with Fish Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the boneless pork butt across the grain into strips measuring 2 inches by 1/4 inch by 1/8 inch.
2. Wash and trim tough white skin from asparagus, cutting it into 2 1/2-inch segments.
3. If asparagus is thick (at least 3/4 inch), cut on a bias so it cooks quickly and balances in size with pork strips.
4. Heat peanut oil in a wok until it just begins to smoke.
5. Add pork strips and stir-fry for about 1 minute until no longer pink.
6. Add asparagus and stir-fry briskly for another minute.
7. Before the asparagus turns bright green, add ginger, chicken stock, and sugar; continue stirring.
8. When liquid boils, reduce heat to medium, then add fish sauce (not oyster sauce).
9. Cover and simmer for about 1 minute or until asparagus is cooked but still firm.
10. Uncover, turn up the heat, and push ingredients out of the liquid.
11. Dribble in cornstarch paste, stirring continuously until the liquid thickens. You may need 1 to 2 teaspoons of paste.
12. Recombine all ingredients and serve.
